1.text: “The world as we have created it is a process of our thinking. It cannot be changed without changing our thinking.”
1.author: Albert Einstein

2.text: “It is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.”
2.author: J.K. Rowling

3.text: “There are only two ways to live your life. One is as though nothing is a miracle. The other is as though everything is a miracle.”
3.author: Albert Einstein

4.text: “The person, be it gentleman or lady, who has not pleasure in a good novel, must be intolerably stupid.”
4.author: Jane Austen

5.text: “Imperfection is beauty, madness is genius and it's better to be absolutely ridiculous than absolutely boring.”
5.author: Marilyn Monroe

6.text: “Try not to become a man of success. Rather become a man of value.”
6.author: Albert Einstein

7.text: “It is better to be hated for what you are than to be loved for what you are not.”
7.author: André Gide

8.text: “I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.”
8.author: Thomas A. Edison

9.text: “A woman is like a tea bag; you never know how strong it is until it's in hot water.”
9.author: Eleanor Roosevelt

10.text: “A day without sunshine is like, you know, night.”
10.author: Steve Martin

11.text: “I love you without knowing how, or when, or from where. I love you simply, without problems or pride: I love you in this way because I do not know any other way of loving but this, in which there is no I or you, so intimate that your hand upon my chest is my hand, so intimate that when I fall asleep your eyes close.”
11.author: Pablo Neruda

12.text: “For every minute you are angry you lose sixty seconds of happiness.”
12.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

13.text: “If you judge people, you have no time to love them.”
13.author: Mother Teresa

14.text: “Anyone who thinks sitting in church can make you a Christian must also think that sitting in a garage can make you a car.”
14.author: Garrison Keillor

15.text: “Beauty is in the eye of the beholder and it may be necessary from time to time to give a stupid or misinformed beholder a black eye.”
15.author: Jim Henson

16.text: “Today you are You, that is truer than true. There is no one alive who is Youer than You.”
16.author: Dr. Seuss

17.text: “If you want your children to be intelligent, read them fairy tales. If you want them to be more intelligent, read them more fairy tales.”
17.author: Albert Einstein

18.text: “It is impossible to live without failing at something, unless you live so cautiously that you might as well not have lived at all - in which case, you fail by default.”
18.author: J.K. Rowling

19.text: “Logic will get you from A to Z; imagination will get you everywhere.”
19.author: Albert Einstein

20.text: “One good thing about music, when it hits you, you feel no pain.”
20.author: Bob Marley

21.text: “The more that you read, the more things you will know. The more that you learn, the more places you'll go.”
21.author: Dr. Seuss

22.text: “Of course it is happening inside your head, Harry, but why on earth should that mean that it is not real?”
22.author: J.K. Rowling

23.text: “The truth is, everyone is going to hurt you. You just got to find the ones worth suffering for.”
23.author: Bob Marley

24.text: “Not all of us can do great things. But we can do small things with great love.”
24.author: Mother Teresa

25.text: “To the well-organized mind, death is but the next great adventure.”
25.author: J.K. Rowling

26.text: “All you need is love. But a little chocolate now and then doesn't hurt.”
26.author: Charles M. Schulz

27.text: “We read to know we're not alone.”
27.author: William Nicholson

28.text: “Any fool can know. The point is to understand.”
28.author: Albert Einstein

29.text: “I have always imagined that Paradise will be a kind of library.”
29.author: Jorge Luis Borges

30.text: “It is never too late to be what you might have been.”
30.author: George Eliot
